decision_summary: Constraints are satisfied with a large margin (43.2°C vs 85.0°C limit). Per optimization history, geometry changes have stalled and hit legality limits. The strategy shifts to material tuning to extract remaining thermal performance gains without risking geometric violations.

changes.reason: Increasing spreader conductivity enhances lateral heat spreading, reducing the peak temperature gradient across the chip. This is the primary recommended lever when geometry adjustments stall.

expected_effects: More uniform temperature distribution in the heat spreader layer
risk_notes: Diminishing returns: doubling conductivity may yield smaller temperature drops than previous geometry changes
Material value exceeds typical physical bounds for standard metals, representing an idealized high-conductivity composite
